ETC-744.title: Bring back old toggle in Boolean Prop/Instance
ETC-744.description:
Users did not like the true/false/data button we added. So we want the old toggle back.

![image.png](https://uploads.linear.app/48a03f57-ea0e-4dba-b596-2bf09659f4d1/70111c36-cb3c-41f8-b2d3-6c9cfff0b2c7/9f329e29-cf7b-40ae-a11d-1ee0ce9726aa)


AC: 

* The user sees a toggle for the boolean prop in editor and in instance (in place of the current button)
* He can use that toggle to switch between true/false (the input follows with "{true}" and "{false}")
* He can write dynamic data into the input, which will disable the toggle
  * (everything except "{true}" and "{false}" should be treated as dynamic data and disable the toggle)
* Removing the text from the input should reenable the toggle and revert back to true/false state. (Default: false)

ETC-737.title: Migrate EtchConditionBlock gutenberg↔etch conversion to PHP
ETC-737.description:
Adds PHP gutenberg↔etch conversion for `etch/condition`, with fixtures and roundtrip coverage.

Depends on: [ETC-729](https://linear.app/digital-gravy/issue/ETC-729/establish-gutenbergetch-json-fixture-corpus-and-generator) (fixture corpus), [ETC-730](https://linear.app/digital-gravy/issue/ETC-730/build-php-gutenbergetch-conversion-infrastructure) (PHP infrastructure).

AC:

* Fixtures committed for `etch/condition`: zero-case (minimal payload), typical case, and block-specific edge cases
* PHP gutenberg → etch matches the etch side of every `etch/condition` fixture
* PHP etch → gutenberg matches the gutenberg side of every `etch/condition` fixture
* Roundtrip `gutenberg → etch → gutenberg` is stable for every `etch/condition` fixture
* Roundtrip `etch → gutenberg → etch` is stable for every `etch/condition` fixture
* Converter registered in the PHP registry for `etch/condition`

ETC-723.title: Allow `create_posts` to create posts of any post type
ETC-723.description:
Currently, the `create_posts` tool hardcodes `post_type` to `"post"` (on the plugin side — `createPosts:19`), preventing the AI from creating entries for custom post types. 

We want to: 

1. loosen the schema to accept any post type slug, so that the AI can create posts for any post type.
2. make this tool more explicit, by mandating a post type to be explicitly passed (no fallback), and requiring the AI to call `list_post_types` before it writes to ensure it uses a valid one.

**Acceptance criteria:**

- [ ] When the AI is in Ask mode, it is not aware of the tool and cannot call it (no regression). 
- [ ] When `create_posts` is called without a `post_type`, it returns an `INVALID_ARGS` error with a specific, explicit error message to the AI model (so it can self-correct).
- [ ] When `create_posts` is called with an invalid `post_type` (i.e. non-existent or reserved), it returns an `INVALID_ARGS` error with a specific, explicit error message to the AI model (so it can self-correct).
- [ ] When `create_posts` is called with a valid `post_type` value (e.g. `page`, `project`), the post is created with that type.
- [ ] After posts are created, the post list for the correct post type is refreshed in the store.

**Implementation notes:**

1. **Schema** (`createPosts.ts:19`) — change `post_type: z.literal('post').optional()` to `post_type: z.string()` (required, any string)
2. **Tool description** — add the MANDATORY `list_post_types` prerequisite language, following the same pattern as `create_etch_loop`:

   ```
   **MANDATORY**: a fresh read of existing post types (`list_post_types`) **must** be performed before calling this tool. NEVER omit `post_type`.
   ```
3. **Post list refresh** (`createPosts.ts:81–84`) — update to refresh the post type matching the created post's type (no hardcoding)

ETC-726.title: Negotiate supported client tools between plugin and middleware
ETC-726.description:
Currently, the middleware advertises a fixed list of client tools to OpenAI regardless of which tools the plugin version in the user's browser actually supports. 

When OpenAI calls an unsupported tool, it silently fails and the AI features in Etch appear **broken**.

This change adds capability negotiation: the plugin sends its supported tool names with every initial request, and the middleware only exposes the intersection to OpenAI. Old plugin versions that don't send the field fall back to a known-safe default set.

**Acceptance Criteria**

- [X] If I'm using an old version of the plugin (1.4.15 — meaning, `supported_client_tools` is not declared), the AI provider receives a hardcoded “classic tools” set: `rag_retrieve`, `docsWebSearch`, `list_post_types`, `create_etch_loop`.
- [X] When I send a message to AI, only client tools the plugin declares support for (in `supported_client_tools`) are offered to the AI provider
  - [X] Tools only present on the plugin side are dropped in the negotiation
  - [X] Tools only present on the server side are dropped in the negotiation
- [X] When the plugin declares an empty set of supported tools (`supported_client_tools = []`), the middleware doesn't accept it and returns an error to the plugin.
  (error code: `EMPTY_SUPPORTED_TOOLS`)
- [X] If the plugin sends a malformed `supported_client_tools`, the middleware doesn't accept it and returns an error to the plugin.
  (error code: `MALFORMED_SUPPORTED_TOOLS`)
- [X] If the plugin sends only unsupported tools (`available_tools = []` after intersection):
  * The middleware blocks the request and returns an error to the plugin 
    (error code `NO_TOOLS_INTERSECTION`)
- [X] If the provider sends a call for a non-declared tool:
  - [X] The middleware blocks the request and returns an error back to the provider, indicating the tool is not supported by this client.
  - [X] The plugin blocks the tool call on the way back, and creates an error 
    (error code `UNDECLARED_TOOL_CALLED`)
  - [X] We display an error toast to the user:
    “There was an issue with this request (UNDECLARED_TOOL_CALLED). Please contact support.”
  - [X] The plugin logs the failure to the Etch activity log
- [ ] ~~Other requests in the same “user turn” (~~`~~continue~~`~~ requests) are unaffected~~

**Implementation notes**

* When a new tool handler is introduced on the plugin side, it is automatically included in the negotiation — no other manual change required.

5 touch points across both repos according to Claude (this could be outdated, keeping it in case it's still partly useful):

1. `aiClientTools.ts` (etch) — export the handler key list (already `Object.keys(handlers)`, just needs exporting)
2. `AiChat.svelte.ts` (etch) — add `supported_client_tools` to the initial request payload; omit on `continue`
3. `AiRoutes.php` (etch) — verify `supported_client_tools` is not stripped by body sanitization
4. `schema.ts` (etch-middleware) — add `supported_client_tools?: string[]` as optional to `InitialRequestSchema`
5. `service.ts` (etch-middleware) — filter client tools against `supported_client_tools` when present; absent → default set; empty → no client tools; server tools always pass through

ETC-730.title: Build PHP gutenberg↔etch conversion infrastructure
ETC-730.description:
PHP-side scaffolding mirroring `blockRegistry.ts` (`src/frontend/Builder/src/composables/blocks/BaseClasses/blockRegistry.ts`). No per-block converters yet — those land in the per-block tickets.

Depends on: [ETC-729](https://linear.app/digital-gravy/issue/ETC-729/establish-gutenbergetch-json-fixture-corpus-and-generator) (fixture corpus).

AC:

* `BlockConverterRegistry` (PHP) exposes `register($type, $converter)` and dispatchers `gutenberg_to_etch($gutenberg_block)` / `etch_to_gutenberg($etch_json)`
* Abstract base converter class defines the per-block contract (e.g. `to_etch($gutenberg_block)`, `to_gutenberg($etch_json)`)
* Dispatchers recurse into children automatically — block converters do not have to walk children themselves
* Dispatchers throw a clear error on unknown block type (mirrors JS `Unknown block type: …`)
* Test helper consumes fixtures from the corpus established in [ETC-729](https://linear.app/digital-gravy/issue/ETC-729/establish-gutenbergetch-json-fixture-corpus-and-generator)
* Registry is empty until per-block tickets register their converters
* `bun run test:php` (phpunit + phpstan + phpcs) passes

Out of scope: any per-block converter implementation.

ETC-721.title: “No response received from AI” on server but works locally
ETC-721.description:
The AI feature consistently returns an error on the staging site, even with a valid API key is used. 

The same site works correctly when cloned locally, which suggests the problem is related to the server environment or the access chain to the site.

@jeremy reproduced the issue directly on 2 sites affected by this problem, including with a known working API key. Both times, cloning the site locally solved the issue instantly.

### Steps to reproduce

1. Open the staging site admin using the provided login link.
2. Enter a valid API key
3. try to send any message to AI

---

### Environment

Hosting: [Infomaniak](<https://infomaniak.com/>) (Switzerland) — managed VPS

Server Type: Managed VPS

Cloudflare: No

Firewall: No known firewall configured by the reporter; there may be a default Infomaniak firewall

Plugin Version: 1.4.15

---

### Magic Login

[Temporary admin login](<https://staging.digitalera.ch/wp-admin/?wtlwp_token=6f3563fcd80cb361260f1660b3cc3b52adaeb1c94f98482ff1b5ad4d2bc91309c6902e3c4d9ddbcb5fe8e23de9bbc6421bd0634f7847da97f888553a887b3640>)

---

### Attachments

[Community post](<https://community.etchwp.com/c/announcements/1-4-8#comment_wrapper_99876808>)

[Support ticket](<https://app.thrivedesk.com/conversations/a1a4cb01-c9d5-47af-b325-83a0447e10a5/11065>)

---

### Reporter

Name: Adnan Hrnic

Email: [contact@digitalera.ch](<mailto:contact@digitalera.ch>)

ETC-712.title: Migrate EtchFocusBlock to Etch JSON
ETC-712.description:
It should inherit the shared data from toBaseJSON from the EtchBaseBlock

AC:

* The JSON includes only the shared data from toBaseJSON (runtime callbacks are not serialized)
* A Zod schema defines the EtchFocusBlock JSON shape (used for typing; validated on fromJSON, not on every toJSON)
* A FocusBlock can be constructed via fromJSON from a valid `etch/focus` JSON payload, returning an `EtchFocusBlock` instance

ETC-727.title: Block registry + dispatch tests
ETC-727.description:
Foundation work for the new JSON serialization: the central `blockRegistry` module, recursive children deserialization, and the dispatcher's own behaviour tests. Per-block round-trip coverage is delivered by each block's migration ticket ([ETC-703](https://linear.app/digital-gravy/issue/ETC-703/migrate-etchhtmlblock-to-etch-json)–716), not here.

## What this ticket delivers

* `blockRegistry` module exposing `registerBlockType(type, klass)` and `fromJSON(json: unknown): EtchBaseBlock`.
* Self-registration pattern: subclasses call `registerBlockType('etch/x', X)` at the bottom of their own file. The registry never value-imports subclasses, which avoids the circular-import problem (registry → subclass → base → registry) the old eager dispatch table caused.
* `composables/blocks/index.ts` barrel — the official first-party block roster, side-effect-imported from `main.ts` so all blocks are registered before `fromJSON` is called.
* `EtchBaseBlock.fromBaseJSON` updated to deserialize children recursively via the registry (previously omitted children).
* Children-handling in the base schema: `looseObject` for child entries (top-level base block stays strict). Preserves subclass-only fields like `tag`/`attributes` so the registry can revalidate each child against its concrete schema.

## Tests

Registry's own contract is covered in `blockRegistry.test.ts`:

* Cross-type dispatch (`etch/element` containing `etch/text` round-trips and each child comes back as the right subclass with data intact).
* Unknown block type throws with a clear error.
* Missing type discriminator throws.

Per-block round-trip tests stay in each block's own test file and land with each block's migration. See [comment](<#>) for the testing approach decision.

## Deviations from original AC

* **No** `satisfies Record<EtchBlockType, EtchBlockClass>` for compile-time exhaustiveness. Self-registration is incompatible with central exhaustiveness — replaced by a runtime "Unknown block type" throw. Trade made consciously to break the import cycle.
* **No** `EtchBlockClass` interface export. Replaced by structural typing inside `registerBlockType`'s generic constraint (`klass: { fromJSON(json: TJSON): EtchBaseBlock }`).
* **Round-trip tests for all 14 blocks** moved out of this ticket. Each migration ticket ([ETC-703](https://linear.app/digital-gravy/issue/ETC-703/migrate-etchhtmlblock-to-etch-json)–716) owns its own round-trip test. This ticket only covers the dispatcher's contract.

ETC-705.title: Migrate EtchTextBlock to Etch JSON
ETC-705.description:
It should inherit the shared data from toBaseJSON from the EtchBaseBlock

AC:

* The JSON must include
  * text content
* A Zod schema defines the EtchTextBlock JSON shape (used for typing; validated on fromJSON, not on every toJSON)
* A TextBlock can be constructed via fromJSON from a valid `etch/text` JSON payload, returning an `EtchTextBlock` instance with the correct text value

ETC-703.title: Migrate EtchHtmlBlock to Etch JSON
ETC-703.description:
It should inherit the shared data from toBaseJSON from the EtchBaseBlock

AC:

* The JSON must include
  * tag
  * attributes
  * styles
* A Zod schema defines the EtchHtmlBlock JSON shape (used for typing; validated on fromJSON, not on every toJSON)
* An HtmlBlock can be constructed via fromJSON from a valid `etch/element` JSON payload, returning an `EtchHtmlBlock` instance with the correct tag, attributes, and styles

ETC-702.title: Migrate EtchBaseBlock to new JSON Structure
ETC-702.description:
The base block provides the foundation for all block toJSON/fromJSON in the Etch JSON format.

AC:

* `EtchBaseBlock` exposes a protected `toBaseJSON()` method that returns the shared data between all blocks as JSON
  * The output must contain only JSON primitives (no class instances, no functions, no Svelte state proxies — must round-trip through `JSON.stringify`/`JSON.parse`)
  * The shared shape includes:
    * children (recursively calling `toJSON` on each child)
    * block context
    * javascript attached to block
    * block options
    * `type` discriminator (added per-call via the typed `toBaseJSON<T extends EtchBlockType>(type: T)` signature so subclasses pass their literal in one place)
* A base Zod schema `EtchBaseBlockJSONSchema` defines the shared shape including the `type` discriminator (used for typing + runtime validation at the fromJSON boundary, not on every toJSON)
* `EtchBaseBlock` declares `abstract toJSON(): EtchBaseBlockJSON` so every subclass must return a typed JSON
* `EtchBaseBlock` exposes a protected static `fromBaseJSON(json)` helper that returns the shared scalar fields (`context`, `script`, `options`) for subclass `fromJSON` implementations to spread. Children are intentionally omitted — the registry ticket wires them up
* toJSON/fromJSON is behind a feature flag

Follow-up (separate ticket): the umbrella `fromJSON` factory + registry + round-trip tests for all block types are captured in [ETC-727](https://linear.app/digital-gravy/issue/ETC-727/implement-block-registry-for-fromjson-dispatch).

ETC-697.title: Cleanup old Component Editor Redesign related flags
ETC-697.description:
* Clean these flags out

  * "ENABLE_CONDITION_PROP": "on",
  * "COMPONENT_INSTANCE_INPUTS_REDESIGN": "on",
  * "PROPS_PANEL_REDESIGN": "on",

* Also remove the experimental toggles for the redesign in the settings.

* Restructure file structure to be flat (remove "new" directory)
